heading: Relationship to other motor vehicle standards
label: 32511 Relationship to other motor vehicle standards
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)32511(a)15:1920(a)., , .Oct. 20, 1972Pub. L. 92–513, § 11086 Stat. 95532511(b)15:1920(b)(1).32511(c)15:1920(b)(2).In subsection (a), the words “may prescribe or enforce . . . only if the standard is identical” are substituted for “no . . . shall have any authority to establish or enforce with respect to . . . which is not identical” to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “a standard prescribed under ” are substituted for “Federal bumper standard” for clarity.section 32502 of this titleIn subsection (b), before clause (1), the words “to continue” are omitted as surplus. The words “a bumper standard about an aspect of performance . . . not covered by a standard prescribed under ” are substituted for “Until a Federal bumper standard takes effect with respect to an aspect of performance” and “any bumper standard which is applicable to the same aspect of performance of such vehicle or item of equipment” to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “if the State bumper standard” are added for clarity.section 32502 of this titleIn subsection (c), the words “that imposes additional or higher standards of performance than” are substituted for “which is not identical to . . . if such requirement imposes an additional or higher standard of performance” for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words.

The words “a bumper standard about an aspect of performance . . . not covered by a standard prescribed under ” are substituted for “Until a Federal bumper standard takes effect with respect to an aspect of performance” and “any bumper standard which is applicable to the same aspect of performance of such vehicle or item of equipment” to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “if the State bumper standard” are added for clarity.section 32502 of this titleIn subsection (c), the words “that imposes additional or higher standards of performance than” are substituted for “which is not identical to . . . if such requirement imposes an additional or higher standard of performance” for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.heading: .—Preemption
children.label: a .—Preemption
children.text: Except as provided in this section, a State or a political subdivision of a State may prescribe or enforce a bumper standard for a passenger motor vehicle or passenger motor vehicle equipment only if the standard is identical to a standard prescribed under .section 32502 of this title
does not conflict with a standard prescribed under chapter 301 of this title; and
was in effect or prescribed by the State on .October 20, 1972

children.heading: .—Additional and Higher Standards of Performance
children.label: c .—Additional and Higher Standards of Performance
children.text: The United States Government, a State, or a political subdivision of a State may prescribe a bumper standard for a passenger motor vehicle or passenger motor vehicle equipment obtained for its own use that imposes additional or higher standards of performance than a standard prescribed under .section 32502 of this title
